trajectory.description: Observation: LightGBM error due to special JSON characters in feature names like "('feature', 'KMID')". Analysis: The MultiIndex column tuples contain parentheses, commas, quotes that LightGBM rejects. Need to flatten column names to simple strings like 'KMID'. Decision: Rename columns to use only the second level of MultiIndex before passing to LightGBM, and save mapping for predict.py.

trajectory.description: Observation: LightGBM stops at iteration 8 with IC 0.0494. The model is overfitting extremely quickly. The custom IC eval function likely has ordering issues - LightGBM may reorder data internally, causing the IC calculation to be incorrect. Analysis: Two problems: 1) IC eval function references X_valid globally but LightGBM may reorder predictions, 2) Insufficient regularization. Decision: Fix IC eval by using MAE for early stopping (reliable), add much more regularization, lower learning rate, and manually compute IC after training to select best iteration.

trajectory.description: Observation: LightGBM baseline gets IC=0.0485 with early stopping on MAE at iteration 198. IC was still improving when MAE plateaued. Analysis: 1) MAE early stopping doesn't optimize IC directly - need IC-based iteration selection. 2) Ensemble of diverse models (LightGBM + CatBoost) should improve robustness. 3) Better hyperparameters (more leaves, different regularization) may help. Decision: Train 2 LightGBM configs + CatBoost, find best IC iteration for each, try multiple ensemble strategies, save best configuration.

trajectory.description: Observation: Validation IC=0.0744 but test IC=0.0426 - huge gap showing overfitting to validation period. The IC-weighted ensemble overfits by weighting models by validation IC. Analysis: 1) Training on 2008-2020 includes very different market regimes from 2022-2023 test. 2) IC-weighted ensemble amplifies overfitting. 3) Need more diverse models (add XGBoost), robust ensemble (simple/rank average), and possibly shorter training window. Decision: Rewrite train.py with: shorter training period (2016-2020), XGBoost for diversity, Huber/fair loss LightGBM variants, CatBoost, save multiple ensemble configs (simple_avg, rank_avg). Use simple average as default since it's more robust to overfitting.

trajectory.description: Observation: XGBoost failed due to wrong objective name (reg:pseudohuber should be reg:pseudohubererror), CatBoost stopped too early (8 iterations) due to aggressive early stopping on MAE. Previous test IC was 0.0426 with validation IC 0.0744 showing overfitting gap. Analysis: 1) Fix XGBoost objective, 2) Disable CatBoost early stopping and select best IC iteration manually, 3) Use simple/rank average ensemble instead of IC-weighted (which overfits to validation). Decision: Rewrite train.py with fixed XGBoost, better CatBoost config, multiple LightGBM variants, and robust ensemble strategies.

Test IC=0.0426 vs validation IC=0.0744 - large overfitting gap. Analysis: Fix XGBoost bug, add more diverse models, use simple average ensemble (more robust than IC-weighted which overfits to validation). Also try shorter training window and different seeds for diversity. Decision: Rewrite train.py with bug fix, add XGBoost with proper params, train multiple diverse models, save ensemble config with simple average.
